channel.title: BinderBrief Daily
channel.description: Daily commercial insurance and underwriting automation insights.

item.title: AI governance in commercial insurance: why now matters
item.description:
IntellectAI reports that in 2026 commercial insurers are focusing on responsible deployment of AI across underwriting and claims workflows to manage risks such as pricing bias and policy errors. Their approach integrates human-in-the-loop models that provide AI-generated insights with final decision-making by underwriters, supported by explainability and governance frameworks involving underwriting, legal, compliance, and board oversight. This shift improves underwriting efficiency while enabling carriers to maintain trust with brokers and clients, mitigating loss exposure and supporting compliance with regulatory and market expectations.
Name: IntellectAI, Company: IntellectAI, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: AI technology provider specializing in underwriting and claims workflows for commercial insurers

item.title: CFC adds affirmative AI coverage across insurance portfolio
item.description:
CFC, a specialist insurer, has introduced affirmative AI coverage language into seven existing insurance products, including Tech E&O, Professional Liability, and Cyber Proactive Response, without launching a separate AI product. The update embeds explicit wording addressing AI-related exposures such as model hallucinations, AI-generated content, and model drift across these policies. This integration aims to clarify AI risk coverage, aligning underwriting practices with evolving AI technologies and potentially improving underwriting accuracy and risk management for brokers and carriers.
Name: Nick Line, Company: CFC, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Underwriting Officer at CFC

item.title: Estimated 40% of Insurers Using AI Underwriting, Says Sollers Report -
item.description:
Four in ten insurers globally are now using artificial intelligence in underwriting, according to a Sollers Consulting report. The adoption primarily involves AI systems triaging submissions, extracting data from unstructured documents, and supporting quote generation, with integration evolving towards flexible pricing platforms and underwriting workbenches. This adoption improves underwriting efficiency by automating submission intake, enhancing portfolio analysis, and gradually enabling underwriters to focus on complex risk selection, thereby affecting broker margins and carrier capacity.
Name: Jakub Śliwiński, Company: Sollers Consulting, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of Underwriting at Sollers Consulting who comments on AI adoption in underwriting

item.title: Editorial: Brokers adapt as risks evolve
item.description:
Brokers face ongoing challenges amid evolving risks and declining property insurance rates, with some publicly traded brokerage firms seeing stock price drops linked to automation fears and rate pressures. The industry is responding by integrating technology to support brokers and shifting focus toward risk mitigation and emerging sectors such as data center risks. These adaptations aim to preserve broker relevance, improve underwriting outcomes, and sustain commercial broker margins despite market and technological pressures.
Name: Gavin Souter, Company: Business Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article and editorial writer for Business Insurance

item.title: China issues new AI governance rules for insurance and banking sectors
item.description:
The National Financial Regulatory Administration (NFRA) of China issued new AI governance guidelines for the insurance and banking sectors, focusing on compliance, transparency, trustworthiness, and management of AI systems. These rules require financial institutions to ensure their AI applications meet defined standards to mitigate risks and promote responsible use. The impact aims to improve underwriting accuracy and operational oversight, which could enhance carrier efficiency and reduce risk exposure in commercial insurance markets.
Name: AIR team, Company: Asia Insurance Review, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Editorial team providing insurance industry news and analysis

item.title: How to maximize AI opportunities
item.description:
A company called Ivans, led by president Michael Streit, outlines four phases to maximize AI use in commercial insurance, focusing on eliminating manual data transcription and automating workflows. The approach involves digitizing unstructured broker and insured communications using large language models (LLMs), linking multiple data points over time, orchestrating end-to-end underwriting workflows, and embedding these AI capabilities into broker processes. This leads to increased underwriting efficiency, higher straight-through-processing rates, and strengthened broker relationships, which can improve insurer growth without raising operating expenses or compromising risk assessment.
Name: Michael Streit, Company: Ivans, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President of Ivans with experience in private equity and an MBA from Harvard Business School

item.title: YRD: AI-powered diversification boosts technology revenue and insurance growth amid improved risk metrics
item.description:
Yiren Digital Ltd. reported a first quarter 2026 earnings update showing increased revenue diversification, driven by growth in technology income and insurance brokerage, despite a GAAP net loss. The company leverages AI-driven operational efficiency and improved risk metrics while investing strategically in AI across new sectors. These developments improve underwriting accuracy and expand capacity in commercial insurance brokerage, potentially increasing broker margins and carrier efficiency.
Name: N/A, Company: Yiren Digital Ltd., Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: A technology and insurance-focused company integrating AI for revenue diversification and operational improvement

item.title: Mayflower Specialty Launches AI Liability Program -
item.description:
Mayflower Specialty, Ltd. and Hadron launched the first dedicated affirmative artificial intelligence liability insurance program in the United States, covering directors and officers, employment practices, and errors and omissions for enterprises deploying AI. The program uses an auditable AI risk scoring engine aligned with NIST and ISO standards and includes difference-in-conditions and excess layers to fill gaps left by traditional policies. This solution addresses a market gap by providing explicit AI risk coverage, improving underwriting precision and expanding capacity for brokers and carriers managing evolving AI liabilities.
Name: Jeremy Epstein, Company: Mayflower Specialty, Ltd., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Mayflower Specialty Name: Pete Buccola, Company: Hadron, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Group Chief Underwriting Officer of Hadron Name: Mayflower Specialty, Ltd., Company: Mayflower Specialty, Ltd., Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: New York-based managing general agent specializing in AI liability underwriting Name: Hadron, Company: Hadron, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Multinational specialty insurance carrier focusing on delegated authority and AI risk coverage

item.title: The price of proof: insurance policies for an AI-enabled world
item.description:
The article discusses the evolving insurance landscape due to AI integration in business operations, highlighting risks such as systemic failures from shared AI models, cloud infrastructure, and APIs that increase loss accumulation potential. It explains that insurers must assess detailed AI usage, dependencies, and controls within insured firms, requiring explicit policy definitions of covered AI systems, incident reporting, and operational controls to manage systemic risk. This approach affects underwriting and pricing by addressing hidden concentration risks, improving insurer capacity to measure and limit loss synchronization, thus influencing commercial broker margins and carrier risk management.
Name: Arthur Charpentier, Company: Université du Québec à Montréal, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Professor of actuarial science and author of the article discussing insurance challenges related to AI risks

item.title: Insurers rethink policy wording as AI risks rise
item.description:
CFC is introducing affirmative artificial intelligence coverage across seven commercial insurance products, adding explicit AI language to policies including technology errors and omissions, professional liability, and cyber proactive response. This update clarifies coverage by addressing AI risks such as model hallucinations, AI-generated content, and model drift through explicit policy wording rather than implied or silent coverage. The changes aim to reduce uncertainty for brokers and clients during placement and claims, improving coverage certainty and underwriting accuracy amid expanding AI use in business.
Name: Mark Rosanes, Company: Insurance Business, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article on AI risks in insurance Name: Nick Line, Company: CFC, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Underwriting Officer at CFC responsible for AI coverage program development

item.title: AI, Geopolitical Tensions, and Human Error Are Reshaping the Cyber Risk Landscape
item.description:
Lockton released its 2026 Cyber Threat Report showing that AI-driven attacks and non-malicious incidents now make up 25% of cyber incidents, based on data from sources including Munich Re, CrowdStrike, and Verizon. The report details how AI lowers the technical barriers for cybercriminals, enabling faster, more sophisticated attacks, and highlights growing risks from operational disruption, third-party vulnerabilities, and non-breach privacy claims. These evolving risks are prompting underwriters to intensify scrutiny of governance, vendor dependencies, and policy coverage limitations, affecting underwriting efficiencies and widening coverage gaps in the cyber insurance market.
Name: Christine Brown, Company: State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance leading Vermont’s captive insurance regulatory division. Name: Kaj Samsom, Company: State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Commissioner of the Department of Financial Regulation supporting captive insurance leadership. Name: Len Crouse, Company: State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Former Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance at Vermont. Name: David Provost, Company: State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Former Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance at Vermont. Name: Sandy Bigglestone, Company: State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Former Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance at Vermont.

item.title: Reliance Global strengthens leadership team to advance AI insurance initiatives
item.description:
Reliance Global Group, Inc. announced new senior leadership appointments including Judah Korman as COO, Zack Wilder as CTO, and Mordy Beyman as Executive Vice President to advance its artificial intelligence-based insurance product development and agency acquisition platform. The company integrates AI technology to automate administrative processes, support underwriting, and develop AI-powered insurance products designed for risk assessment, pricing, and customer engagement, leveraging data from its acquired independent insurance agencies. This strategy aims to improve operational efficiency, compress integration timelines, and enhance commercial broker margins and carrier underwriting through increased automation and data-driven insights.
Name: Judah Korman, Company: Reliance Global Group, Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Operating Officer responsible for operating model expansion and agency integration. Name: Zack Wilder, Company: Reliance Global Group, Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Technology Officer leading AI product development and technology infrastructure. Name: Mordy Beyman, Company: Reliance Global Group, Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Executive Vice President overseeing strategy coordination and AI roadmap. Name: Moshe Fishman, Company: Reliance Global Group, Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Vice President of Insurtech managing AI-related initiatives. Name: Ezra Beyman, Company: Reliance Global Group, Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Executive Officer guiding the AI-driven acquisition and product strategy. Name: Taylor Mixides, Company: Reinsurance News, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article on Reliance Global Group's leadership and AI initiatives.

item.title: Mitigata raises $15m Series B for AI cyber resilience
item.description:
Mitigata, an AI-native cyber resilience platform based in India, closed a $15 million Series B funding round led by Bessemer Venture Partners with participation from Nexus Venture Partners, Titan Capital, and WEH Ventures. The platform integrates cyber insurance, AI-driven security operations, cyber risk intelligence, compliance automation, and incident response into a single system that serves over 800 organizations and processes more than one million security incidents using AI. This integration reduces cyber risk exposure and financial loss for enterprises while potentially improving underwriting accuracy and operational efficiency for insurers in the cyber insurance market.
Name: Mohit Anand, Company: Mitigata, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and CEO of Mitigata, an AI-native cyber resilience platform. Name: Sarthak Dubey, Company: Mitigata, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Mitigata. Name: Mayank Morya, Company: Mitigata, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Mitigata. Name: Akshit Kaushik, Company: Mitigata, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Mitigata. Name: Pankaj Mitra, Company: Bessemer Venture Partners, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Partner at Bessemer Venture Partners, lead investor in Mitigata’s funding round.

item.title: How insurers should deploy AI without the pitfalls: WTW
item.description:
WTW released insights on how insurers should deploy AI, emphasizing that AI predictions must be stable and explainable rather than solely generative or agentic, with human expertise embedded in the process. Their approach combines traditional models like GLMs with machine learning, supported by constant human oversight to identify data issues and evolve predictive accuracy. This method improves carrier underwriting efficiency and supports more nuanced risk assessment while maintaining the role of subject-matter experts, potentially enhancing productivity without reducing human involvement.
Name: Magdalena Ramada, Company: WTW, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global insurtech innovation leader at WTW Name: Michael Shashoua, Company: Digital Insurance, Email: michael.shashoua@arizent.com, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Editor at Digital Insurance and author of the article

item.title: In the AI rush to automate, what must we not lose?
item.description:
Michael Keating discussed the acceleration of AI adoption in the insurance sector, focusing on the Managing General Agent (MGA) market, without citing specific product releases or funding rounds. He explained that AI is streamlining underwriting and claims processes by automating administrative tasks, improving data analysis, and enabling faster decisions, while raising concerns about maintaining underwriter expertise and accountability. This shift could increase broker productivity and carrier efficiency but requires balancing technology use with ongoing mentorship and governance to preserve industry judgment and relationships.
Name: Michael Keating, Company: N/A, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Insurance industry commentator and columnist focusing on insurance market developments and AI impact

item.title: Majesco points to accelerating AI adoption reshaping insurance operating models
item.description:
Majesco released a 2026 strategic priorities report focused on AI adoption in the insurance industry, highlighting increased investment in artificial intelligence, generative AI, and agentic AI technologies. The report is based on primary research showing that insurers classified as Leaders use cloud and AI-native technology foundations to integrate AI deeply into their operations, enabling automation, intelligence-driven processes, and modernized operating models. This shift improves operational efficiency, lowers expense ratios, enhances underwriting and claims processes, and strengthens the competitive positioning of advanced insurers relative to industry peers.
Name: Denise Garth, Company: Majesco, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Strategy Officer at Majesco responsible for strategic direction and AI integration advocacy

item.title: Cayman Finance promotes the industry at major US insurtech and captive conferences
item.description:
Cayman Finance participated in InsurTech Insights in New York and the US Captive Summit in Chicago to promote the Cayman Islands’ insurance and captive insurance sectors. The organisation engaged with US stakeholders, hosted events, and highlighted the jurisdiction’s regulatory framework and insurtech capabilities through collaboration with the Insurance Managers Association of Cayman (IMAC) and industry panels. These activities aim to sustain and grow the Cayman Islands’ market share as a leading insurance domicile, supporting broker margins and carrier underwriting through enhanced relationships and innovation adoption.
Name: Brittany MacVicar, Company: Cayman Finance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Associate Director for Insurance and Reinsurance at Cayman Finance Name: Haymond Rankin, Company: Cayman Finance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Associate Director for Banking, Fintech and Virtual Assets at Cayman Finance Name: James Trundle, Company: Insurance Managers Association of Cayman (IMAC), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chair of the Insurance Managers Association of Cayman Name: Kevin Poole, Company: Insurance Managers Association of Cayman (IMAC), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: General Manager of the Insurance Managers Association of Cayman

item.title: Weecover makes case for modular AI in insurance
item.description:
Weecover, an InsurTech platform focused on insurance management and distribution technology, has presented its view that microservices architecture is key to scaling AI deployment in insurance, amid $1.63 billion invested in AI-focused companies in Q1 2026. The platform proposes a modular, cloud-based approach using APIs and middleware to layer new AI capabilities onto existing legacy systems without full core replacements. This method aims to improve insurers’ operational efficiency and data integration, enabling more effective AI adoption and potentially increasing underwriting capacity and commercial broker margins.
Name: Jordi Pagès, Company: Weecover, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Weecover advocating modular AI architecture in insurance systems.

item.title: "Months, not years away": Five Eyes warning puts AI cyber threat on New Zealand's radar
item.description:
New Zealand’s National Cyber Security Centre, led by Catriona Robinson, co-signed a Five Eyes statement warning that AI-powered cyber threats will escalate significantly within months, not years. The warning highlights rapid advancements in frontier AI models capable of transforming cyber attack and defense tactics, with New Zealand banks reporting $265 million in fraud losses related to AI-assisted scams and social engineering. This development pressures insurers and brokers to review cyber policy wordings and coverage to manage increasing risks and avoid gaps, impacting underwriting efficiency and commercial broker margin through potentially higher claims and coverage disputes.
Name: Catriona Robinson, Company: New Zealand National Cyber Security Centre, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of New Zealand’s National Cyber Security Centre and co-signer of a Five Eyes AI cyber threat statement Name: Jeffrey Gonlin, Company: Emergence Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief underwriter at Emergence Insurance who comments on the risk exposure gap related to AI in cyber insurance Name: Casper Rogers, Company: Assured, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Broker at Assured warning about potential insurer exclusions limiting AI-related cyber risk exposure Name: Tim Johnson, Company: Browne Jacobson, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Partner and Head of Insurance at Browne Jacobson law firm discussing challenges in cyber policy definitions around AI attackers Name: Ed Ventham, Company: Assured, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Industry professional advising businesses to explicitly include AI coverage in cyber policies

item.title: Five Eyes just put a deadline on the AI cyber threat - Canadian brokers need to get ahead of it
item.description:
Canada’s Centre for Cyber Security, part of the Five Eyes alliance, issued a joint statement warning that AI-driven cyber threats are accelerating with a timeframe in months. The warning describes AI’s impact on offensive and defensive cyber tools, with AI-enhanced social engineering and phishing attacks drawing on real-world relationship mapping and sophisticated impersonation. This rapid threat evolution is outpacing current cyber insurance pricing models, creating pressure on policy language and increasing the risk of coverage gaps for brokers and carriers.
Name: Rajiv Gupta, Company: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security, Email: N/A, Type: Government, Info: Head of the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security and signatory of the Five Eyes AI cyber threat statement Name: Neal Jardine, Company: BOXX Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Cyber risk director at BOXX Insurance commenting on AI-enhanced cyber attacks Name: Paige Cheasley, Company: Gallagher Canada, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: National technology practice leader at Gallagher Canada discussing AI-related liability in insurance Name: Casper Rogers, Company: Assured, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Broker at Assured warning about potential new exclusionary policy language Name: Tim Johnson, Company: Browne Jacobson, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Partner and Head of Insurance at law firm Browne Jacobson highlighting policy definition concerns on AI attackers Name: Jeffrey Gonlin, Company: Emergence Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief underwriter at Emergence Insurance describing AI as accelerating existing cyber threats Name: Ed Ventham, Company: Assured, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Insurance professional at Assured advising clients to seek affirmative AI coverage in cyber policies
